regime.label: Geopolitical Pivot
regime.sublabel: Energy Shock Fading, Policy Fog Thickening
regime.description: Markets are navigating a sharp de-escalation in the Iran conflict, which drove oil from ~$67 to near $120 before pulling back toward $91–95 on ceasefire signals. Equity indices have recovered strongly from their late-March lows, but the macro backdrop remains cloudy: US CPI is sticky at 3.3%, the Fed is frozen at 3.50–3.75% with a hawkish split on its board, and a Fed Chair transition adds a new layer of institutional uncertainty. The CNN Fear & Greed Index at 47 (Neutral) and VIX at ~17 confirm markets are neither complacent nor panicked — a tentative equilibrium that could break decisively in either direction.

riskMeter.label: Cautiously Neutral
riskMeter.sublabel: Oil pullback and Iran deal hopes are lifting sentiment, but sticky inflation and Fed Chair uncertainty keep a ceiling on risk appetite.

signals.label: S&P 500
signals.note: S&P had its best month since 2020 in April, recovering from Iran-shock lows; AI capex cycle and mega-cap earnings are the primary driver.

signals.label: TSX Composite
signals.note: TSX benefiting from elevated (though falling) oil prices; energy sector strength offsets financials drag. Outperforming EM but lagging S&P YTD.

signals.label: VIX
signals.note: Pulled back sharply from 28 peak in April as Iran ceasefire signals emerged; still above pre-conflict lows of ~13–14, reflecting residual uncertainty.

signals.label: Nasdaq / Tech
signals.note: AI infrastructure theme intact; mega-caps (NVDA, META, AMZN) reported solid Q1. Russell 2000 lagging, confirming large-cap quality bias.

signals.label: Fed Funds Rate
signals.note: Fed held for 3rd consecutive meeting on April 29; 8-4 vote split — most divisive decision since 1992. Markets pricing near-zero chance of cuts in 2026 as oil/inflation risks persist.

signals.label: 10Y US Treasury
signals.note: Pulled back from 9-month high of ~4.45% as Iran deal hopes crushed oil; still elevated and keeping valuation multiples under pressure.

signals.label: Yield Curve (2s10s)
signals.note: Curve has been gradually steepening from deeply inverted levels; near-normal slope suggests growth expectations intact but not exuberant.

signals.label: BoC Rate
signals.note: BoC held April 29; projecting 1.2% GDP growth in 2026. CPI climbed to 2.4% in March on gasoline prices; core remains just above 2%. No cuts expected through 2026.
